title: Smoky Silky Kenyan Herring Stew
description: A smoky, silky stew packed with protein from herring and kid-friendly flavors, perfect for a quick Kenyan dinner.
instructions: Heat a large pot over medium heat and sauté the chopped onion and garlic until translucent.
Add the diced potatoes and carrot, cooking for 4 minutes while stirring.
Stir in the tomato paste, smoked paprika, and ground cumin, cooking for another minute to release flavors.
Pour in the vegetable stock and bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Add the flaked smoked herring fillets and bay leaf, spooning in the coconut milk, and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, removing the bay leaf.
Ladle the stew into bowls, ensuring each child receives a generous portion of silky herring.
Serve hot with a side of plain rice or a slice of crusty bread.
